game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters

design_philosophy.core_loop: Tournament-based 1v1 fighting matches with three-round best-of-three format; fill special meter through offense to unleash powerful super attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering character-specific command moves and timing special attacks versus opponent adaptation across multiple rounds
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic TMNT characters to prove dominance in a fighting tournament and directly confront Shredder as final boss
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Genesis version emphasizes arcade-style tournament structure with accessible four-button fighting scheme; integrates IP narrative (Turtles in tournament) rather than treating fighting game as pure mechanical abstraction

difficulty.continues_system: Adjustable continue system available in options menu
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can adjust difficulty, continues, and game speed in options menu before starting

progression.unlock_mechanism: Story mode progresses through sequential tournament matches; defeat first three opponents (inter-Turtle matchups), then Casey Jones, then Hothead, culminating in Shredder final boss
replayability_hooks: Versus CPU mode with unlimited character matching options
Adjustable difficulty and speed settings encourage replay with different challenge levels

world_building_techniques: Tournament framing device provides context for character encounters
Character archetypes integrated from TMNT Adventures comics (Hothead based on Dragon Warrior)
IP-specific narrative beats (rescuing April O'Neill and Splinter in SNES version)

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic final confrontation with series antagonist; positioned after tournament progression through progressively challenging opponents
notable_bosses: Shredder (Cyber Shredder in SNES version) - final tournament boss representing ultimate challenge and series conflict resolution

faction_indicators: Turtle selection as character choice creates implicit player faction (Leonardo, Raphael, Michaelangelo, Donatello)
danger_signals: Shredder appearance as final boss represents existential threat to Turtle protagonists
Karai referenced in SNES story mode as kidnapper of April and Splinter
environmental_storytelling: Turtle Blimp travel across US in SNES story mode suggests journey/exploration narrative

sources_used: Wikipedia: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Tournament Fighters
